title: Namana
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Species |image= |name=Namana |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Non-sentient]]''[[The Truce at Bakura]]'' |class=[[Tree/Legends|Tree]] |subspecies= |races= |height= |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=[[Color/Legends|Yellow]] [[Wood/Legends|wood]]''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]'' |haircolor= |feathers= |eyecolor= |distinctions=*Slightly addictive [[Fruit/Legends|fruit]] *Used in [[Namana liquor|liquor]], [[Namana candy|candy]] [[Namana cream pie|cream pie]] and [[Namana custard|custard]]{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/dexdiner/index.html|text=Dining at Dex's|int=Dining at Dex's}} |lifespan= |origin=[[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]] |habitat= |diet= |language= }} {{Quote|Namana fruit, once processed, induces a faint sense of pleasure. Most people don't notice immediately. They just feel good without knowing why.|Gaeriel Captison|}} The [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]] was home to the '''namana''', a [[Species/Legends|species]] of tall, straight-trunked [[Tree/Legends|tree]] that was pale yellow in [[Color/Legends|color]]. In addition to copious foliage and colorful blossoms, the tree produced a [[Fruit/Legends|fruit]]. From this fruit, the [[Bakuran]]s produced and exported products such as [[namana liquor]] and [[Namana candy|namana candies]]. These refined products were slightly addictive, stimulating the pleasure centers in [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Brain/Legends|brains]]. Namana-based exports became a major part of the Bakuran economy. In the wild, namana trees attracted both [[Bird/Legends|birds]] and [[cratsch]]; the latter was an arboreal predator that fed on namana-satiated birds. In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Bakura was [[Invasion of Bakura|invaded]] by the [[Ssi-ruu]] species. In order to combat the invaders, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] leadership on Bakura agreed to a truce with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]; this temporary peace was sealed with a handshake over goblets of [[namana nectar]]. The Bakurans later named a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] class, the [[Namana-class light cruiser|''Namana''-class light cruiser]], after the species. Several namana trees also grew on the planet [[Garqi/Legends|Garqi]], in a section of its [[Pesktda Xenobotanical Garden]] that was dedicated to exotic offworld species. These trees were [[Battle of Garqi|burnt down]] by [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] in an attempt to hide from the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] the discovery that [[Vonduun Skerr Kyrric|Vonduun crab armor]] was allergic to pollen from [[bafforr tree]]s, which also grew in the garden. ==Biology and appearance== {{Quote|I take it your message had Karagga quivering in his juices like a namana custard?|Ambassador Rove|{{TORcite|type=operation|Karagga's Palace}}}} The namana was a [[Species/Legends|species]] of tropical [[Tree/Legends|tree]] that was pale yellow in [[Color/Legends|color]]. Its trunk was straight and tall, with gracefully drooping branches that produced delicate blossoms amidst plentiful foliage. The namana tree produced an exotic [[Fruit/Legends|fruit]],[[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']] from which a pale orange [[Namana nectar|nectar]] could be distilled into a [[Namana liquor|liquor]]. This viscous fluid was potable to [[Human/Legends|Humans]]. While it initially produced a burning sensation in the [[Throat/Legends|throat]], this gave way to a flavor reminiscent of jungle [[Plant/Legends|flowers]] and sweet fruit. Namana fruit could also be used to produce yellow-orange [[Namana candy|candies]]. Once processed, consumption of the fruit stimulated the pleasure centers of the Human [[Brain/Legends|brain]]. As a consequence, namana products were slightly addictive. ==History== [[File:Cratsch.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Cratsch defended a territory of namana trees.]] ===Natural history=== The namana evolved on [[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]], a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] with plentiful [[Water/Legends|rainfall]]''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' that was located at the outer edge of the [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] [[Shiritoku Spur]].''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' Bakura was home to several species of [[Bird/Legends|birds]], which were attracted to the namana trees for their fruit. After gorging themselves on the fruit, many birds dropped to the forest floor in a drunken stupor. These birds were then easy prey to the [[cratsch]], a [[ferret]]-like, arboreal predator. [[Gender/Legends|Male]] cratsch marked out large copses of namana trees with musk glands located on their tails. They became particularly territorial of their namana trees during their mating season. ===Cultivation=== {{Quote|Go ahead. It's not toxic. Our finest local product. You're insulting Bakura if you refuse.|Gaeriel Captison offers namana nectar to Luke Skywalker|}} As early as [[3639 BBY]], namana was known of off-Bakura, as it was used as an ingredient in [[namana custard]]. [[Ambassador/Legends|Ambassador]] [[Rove (Ambassador)|Rove]] of the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|resurgent Sith Empire]] referenced the juicy dessert as an idiom for cowardice. Around [[150 BBY/Legends|150 BBY]], Bakura was settled by the [[Bakur Mining Corporation]], which established [[Ore/Legends|ore]] [[Mining/Legends|mines]]. However, as the ore veins began to diminish, the [[Bakuran]] economy began to founder; to counter this, the colonists diversified their exports. While the primary export was [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] technology, the Bakurans also commercially harvested the namana fruit to produce candies and liquor for export.''[[Heroes & Rogues]]'' The fruit was also used in the [[Namana cream pie|cream pies]] and custard desserts that were served at [[Dex's Diner/Legends|Dex's Diner]], a culinary establishment on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. In the period following the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], several namana processing plants were established on Bakura,''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' with numerous plantations on the southernmost part of the main continent, [[Prytis]], and others on the continent [[Braad]]. The namana became a significant cultural icon for the Bakurans, and it was considered an insult to Bakura if an individual refused to drink namana nectar at a significant occasion. Some individuals wore clothing patterned with namana blossoms, while others, such as [[Defense Minister (Bakura)|Defense Minister]] [[Blaine Harris]], used canes made of namana [[Wood/Legends|wood]]. Namana wood was also used in several important Bakuran state buildings. Thick namana wood doors and namana paneling were used throughout the [[Bakur Memorial Building]], the seat of the Bakuran government. Its richly decorated guest quarters also featured yellow namana wood paneling. The office of the Bakuran [[Prime Minister (Bakura)|Prime Minister]] featured three-dimensional, colored [[Glass/Legends|glass]] representations of namana trees framing its [[Window/Legends|windows]]. Namana trees were also found in [[Statuary Park]], landscaped so as to provide the illusion of a large, undisturbed forest—a haven in the bustling [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Salis D'aar/Legends|Salis D'aar]]. ===Truce at Bakura=== {{Quote|Princess Leia, if your troops are willing to cooperate with mine, under truce, we need your help.''"
"''An official truce, Your Excellency?''"
"''As official as I can make it. ''[A toast]'' To strange alliances.|Governor Nereus and Princess Organa declare a truce|}} In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], shortly after the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] victory at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] and the concomitant [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], the Bakurans were attacked by the [[Ssi-ruu]] species, and so sent a distress signal to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Fleet/Legends|Fleet]] at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. There, the message was received by the Rebel Alliance, who sent a small taskforce to aid Bakura in the hope of persuading the planet into the Alliance. [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], as lead Rebel negotiator, received a briefing on Bakura that included information on the economic value of the namana trees. [[File:Bakura_truce.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Over goblets of namana nectar, a truce is declared between the Empire and the Alliance.]] Following a brief skirmish with the Ssi-ruuk upon their arrival, the Rebel contingent attended a banquet at the residence of Bakuran Prime Minister [[Yeorg Captison]], at which several namana-based products were served—both namana nectar and candies. While the Imperials were initially skeptical of the Rebels' proclamation of Palpatine's death, confirmation arrived during the dinner. As a consequence, over a goblet of namana nectar, a historic truce was declared between the Galactic Empire and the Alliance in order to fight the Ssi-ruuk. The Imperial [[Governor/Legends|governor]] who agreed to the truce, [[Wilek Nereus]], was addicted to namana-products. He endeavored to control his habit by limiting his intake to twice-daily namana candy snacks—often chewy [[namana twist]]s eaten near an internal fountain—and only imbibing nectar in the evenings. As the combined defense against the Ssi-ruuk unfolded, Captison and [[Senior Senator of the Republic]] [[Orn Belden]] organized an [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]] ride with Organa, in order to discuss possible links between Bakura and the Alliance. While they traveled to a secure location to speak freely, Captison passed the [[Time/Legends|time]] by discussing innocuous topics, such as namana fruit harvests. However, shortly after they began discussing what it would take to persuade Bakuran civilians to rise up against the Empire, the three were arrested. The arrests sparked an uprising, and knowing the Imperials would try and secure Bakura's repulsorlift factories, Captison's niece, [[Gaeriel Captison]], sliced into Imperial [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]]' information banks and replaced their intelligence on the factory with data for a namana juice extraction plant. This confusion was compounded by Ssi-ruuvi forces landing on the surface and [[Invasion of Bakura|capturing]] Rebel [[Commander/Legends|commander]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. Skywalker had earlier been infected with [[Olabrian trichoid]]s by Nereus, and on hearing of his capture the Imperial celebrated with namana nectar, believing that the trichoids would hatch inside Skywalker and later infest the Ssi-ruuk. His celebration was premature, however, as Skywalker escaped the Ssi-ruuk, and the Rebels defeated both the Ssi-ruuvi forces and, later, the Imperial forces, who broke the truce following the Ssi-ruuvi defeat. Shortly thereafter, [[Bix Arden-Govia]], the progeny of two significant Bakuran families, returned to Bakura after cavaliering about the Shiritoku Spur and purchased his own namana plantation before reentering Salis D'aar high society. Several namana trees were also grown offworld in the [[Pesktda Xenobotanical Garden]] of [[Garqi/Legends|Garqi]]. The garden had a complex of greenhouses dedicated to offworld trees, which included a grove of [[bafforr tree]]s from [[Ithor/Legends|Ithor]]. In the following [[Standard year/Legends|years]], the Bakurans developed several [[Starship/Legends|starship]] classes, naming one the [[Namana-class light cruiser|''Namana''-class light cruiser]].''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]'' ===Yuuzhan Vong War=== {{Quote|If I had to guess, their living armor had a nasty allergic reaction to this pollen. It's swollen up and is killing them. We have to burn this all down. Everything.|Jedi Knight Corran Horn orders the Pesktda Xenobotanical Garden to be burnt down|''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide II: Ruin]]''}} In [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invaded]] [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. During a mission to Garqi, several [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] ventured into the Pesktda Xenobotanical Garden and discovered that the [[Vonduun Skerr Kyrric|Vonduun crab armor]] worn by the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] invaders was allergic to bafforr pollen. In order to hide such a militarily significant discovery from the Yuuzhan Vong, the Garqi [[Resistance (Garqi)|Resistance]] [[Battle of Garqi|burned down]] the entire Xenobotanical Garden, including its namana specimens. [[28 ABY/Legends|Three years]] later, [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]] affiliate [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and his now-[[Marriage/Legends|wife]], Leia Organa Solo, returned to Bakura with their daughter [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina]] on a [[Battle of Bakura (28 ABY)|mission]] to reestablish contact with the Bakurans. There, at a formal dinner, namana nectar was served. Jaina, who had long heard about the sweet nectar, sampled the liquor, although not enough to dull her senses.''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic II: Refugee]]'' ==Behind the scenes== Author [[Kathy Tyers]] created the namana tree for her novel ''[[The Truce at Bakura]]'', published by [[Bantam Spectra]] in [[1994]]. Bakura, on which the story largely unfolded, was included in several reference books following the novel's release, often accompanied by a mention of its namana products. These works included three [[Star Wars: Essential Guides|''Essential Guides'']], the ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', and, [[2008|ten years later]], its successor, ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', all from [[Del Rey]]; ''[[Heroes & Rogues]]'' and ''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]'' by [[West End Games]], with the latter product significantly expanding on the namana trees; and [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''. Namana-based produced were also featured in "[[Dining at Dex's]]," an [[in-universe]] menu for Dex's Diner, and referenced in the [[Star Wars video games|MMORPG]] ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Onslaught]]'' {{Mo}} *[[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']] {{Mo}} *''[[The Truce at Bakura]]'' {{1st}} *[[The Truce at Bakura (abridged audiobook)|''The Truce at Bakura'' abridged audiobook]] *{{WEGCite|story=Second Pledge|book=The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook}} {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[Heroes & Rogues]]'' *''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]'' *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]'' *''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/dexdiner/index.html|text=Dining at Dex's|int=Dining at Dex's}} ==Notes and references== {{ScrollBox|height=400px|content={{Reflist}}}} {{Interlang |de=Namana |fr=Namana }} [[Category:Pharmaceutical plants]] [[Category:Plants of Bakura]] [[Category:Trees]]
